summary: The reproduction bundle builds n8n from source, starts the server with an active webhook workflow, and sends a large multipart upload to the webhook endpoint to demonstrate unbounded resource consumption without authentication.
expected_security_impact: The n8n server should accept and buffer a large multipart upload (well beyond the 16 MiB N8N_PAYLOAD_SIZE_MAX) at a webhook endpoint, demonstrating that the 200 MiB per-file limit applies but no aggregate size limit or rate limiting protects webhook endpoints.
actual_observed_result: PoC was not executed in this session. The reproduction bundle was generated based on code analysis confirming the vulnerable path exists.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sends a multipart upload with multiple 50 MiB parts to a webhook endpoint and checks that the server accepts the upload without error, confirming no aggregate size limit is enforced.
limitations_or_blockers: PoC was not executed due to time and resource constraints. Building n8n from source requires Node.js 22+, pnpm 10+, and significant build time. The vulnerability was confirmed through static code analysis of the formidable configuration and middleware ordering.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
